title: Frontier doctrine RFC and invariant amendments
goal: Implement the AF-00 roadmap workpackage. Create docs/design/frontier-autonomy.md as an execution-grade RFC for autonomous frontier behavior, using docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md and docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/01-doctrine.md as the source plan. The RFC must reconcile autonomy with the existing replan authority channel: candidate follow-up slices are replan proposals, the mission envelope delegates only decision-recording authority, and one idempotent apply engine serves both operator and envelope-authorized accepts. Append proposed Phase-style invariant amendment records to docs/workflow-invariants.md. Keep the language obvious and explicit; avoid introducing names that imply a second workflow owner.
acceptance: docs/design/frontier-autonomy.md exists and defines MissionEnvelope fields and validation, tier semantics with stable reason codes, the autonomy authority ladder and evidence bar, stop rules, provenance requirements, and deferral reopenings.
The RFC states that follow-up candidates use the existing replan proposal/decision/apply channel and that no separate frontier authority channel exists.

docs/workflow-invariants.md contains proposed amendment records in the existing Phase-style format, including enforcement mechanisms, violation-detecting tests, and status for mission envelopes, envelope-delegated auto-acceptance, and generated follow-up slice provenance.
AF-00 remains docs-only: no Rust, JavaScript, schema, script, or runtime behavior changes are made.
Roadmap truth lint still passes for docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md.
must_ask_if: If completing AF-00 appears to require runtime/schema/code changes, stop and ask instead of changing code.
If the RFC cannot pick explicit N/M/X evidence-bar numbers for promote/run authority, ask the operator rather than leaving the bar vague.
If the existing replan channel appears insufficient and a second authority channel seems necessary, stop and ask before documenting that direction.
